text:
In a quiet village hidden among rolling hills lived a fox named Felix. Felix loved exploring outdoors. One fine autumn morning, he embarked on an unusual adventure. Seeking something sweet and crunchy, Felix ventured into an old oak forest.

Deep behind the protective woody screen stood an old maple tree. Its branches hung low. Felix knew it held secrets. He scaled upwards. Higher. Higher still.

Suddenly, Felix heard familiar chirps somewhere deeper. Looking upwards, he spotted a family of birds. They were singing like mad!

"Aww!" Felix thought.

What mischief could birds want? He flew closer.

Here laid an old stone sculpture. Oddly crafted. Wings outstretched.

With a wink from an owl at his lofty perch, Felix couldn't resist. He swooped low and tossed the owl’s wings.

They fluttered wildly.

For a moment nothing happened.

Suddenly, the owl realized its wings were airborne.

It squawked outrageously. Birds swooped towards Felix.
